headline: GMIN ACQUIRES G2 Goldfields For C$3 Billion — 500koz/yr Tier-1 Hub In Guyana At 72% Premium
snippet: G Mining Ventures (GMIN) will acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal at a 72% premium, consolidating adjacent Oko West and Oko-Ghanie projects in Guyana into a combined operation targeting 500koz/yr gold with over C$1 billion in quantified synergies.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver HITS 4,896 g/t Over 1.3m At Galena — 10 New Veins Found, Largest-Ever 64,000m Drill Program Launched
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (USAS) discovered ten new high-grade veins at its Idaho Galena Complex, highlighted by 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company has launched its largest-ever 64,000m drill campaign across Galena and Cosalá for 2026.

headline: COPPER MARKET Enters First Structural Deficit Since 2009 — ICSG Forecasts 150,000t Shortage As Grasberg Stays Closed
snippet: The ICSG has abandoned its projected surplus and now forecasts a 150,000-tonne refined copper deficit for 2026 — the first structural shortage since 2009. The Grasberg Block Cave, supplying 70% of mine output, remains closed after a fatal September mudslide, while AI data center demand adds 475kmt of new consumption.

headline: Gold Holds Near $4,750 As Dollar Cracks Below DXY 100 — GDX Returns 108% Year-Over-Year, Miners Finally Outpacing The Metal
snippet: Gold is holding near $4,750/oz as the DXY slips below 100 on dollar weakness and geopolitical uncertainty. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) has returned 108% year-over-year, with RBC upgrading Kinross Gold to Outperform with a $45 target citing 11%+ free cash flow yield.

headline: SILVER Near $75/oz With China Buying 790+ Tonnes — Annual Structural Deficit Headed For 200 Million Oz In 2026
snippet: Silver is up 134% year-over-year and hovering near $75/oz, buoyed by China's 790-tonne import surge and solar demand. The 2026 annual deficit is forecast to reach 200 million ounces as China export constraints tighten physical supply further.

headline: Ex-Rio CEO Tom Albanese Chairs $1B Deep-Sea Merger — AOMC + Odyssey Marine Target Nickel, Cobalt And Copper Nodules In Pacific
snippet: American Ocean Minerals (AOMC) and Odyssey Marine Exploration (OMEX) have agreed to a $1B all-stock merger, backed by $150M in institutional financing. Former Rio Tinto CEO Tom Albanese will chair the combined entity, which targets polymetallic nodules in the Cook Islands EEZ.

headline: URANIUM Retreats To $92/lb After Jan Peak Of $101 — AI Data Centers Reshape Nuclear Demand, Mined Supply Covers Under 75% Of Reactor Needs
snippet: Uranium spot has cooled to $92/lb after touching $101 in January, but structural demand from AI-driven data centers is reshaping the long-term thesis. The U.S. DOE committed $2.7 billion to domestic enrichment capacity, while mined supply is forecast to cover under 75% of future reactor requirements.

headline: Lithium DOUBLES In Q1 2026 As Zimbabwe Bans Raw Mineral Exports — China Prices Hit CNY 160,000/T, Up 40% Year-To-Date
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate surged from $13,433/t to $26,278/t in Q1 2026 as Zimbabwe — supplying 7% of global LCE — abruptly banned raw mineral exports. China spot prices crossed CNY 160,000/t, up 40% YTD, driven by 22% EV sales growth and a 55% expected jump in storage demand.

headline: Fathom Nickel Drills NEW Ni-Cu-Co Discovery 500m From Historic Gochager Lake Deposit — Strongest BHEM Conductor Yet Identified
snippet: Fathom Nickel (CSE:FNI) intersected broad magmatic Ni-Cu-Co sulphide zones 500m ENE of the historic Gochager Lake deposit during its winter program. The company identified its strongest-ever off-hole BHEM conductor 1.6km to the ENE; full assays expected mid-June 2026.

headline: Troilus Mining Launches 40,000m Campaign In Quebec — Z87 High-Grade Underground And Regional Targets Across 435 km² Land Package
snippet: Troilus Mining (TSX:TLG) has kicked off a 40,000m drill program at its copper-gold Troilus Project in north-central Quebec, targeting the Z87 high-grade underground zone (15,000m), hanging wall upside (10,000m), and regional priority targets. A 2024 feasibility study supports a 22-year, 50ktpd open-pit operation.

headline: Ghana's SLIDING-SCALE Gold Royalty (5%–12%) Draws Rare China-US Joint Pushback — Miners Warn Investment Will Flee
snippet: Ghana is pushing ahead with a sliding-scale gold royalty of 5%–12% pegged to bullion prices, replacing the current fixed 3%–5% rate. China and the US have jointly lobbied Accra to halt the hike, while the Ghana Chamber of Mines warns the policy will choke the $500M–$600M in capital needed to develop a single new mine.

headline: Weiss Ratings Declares END Of Nine-Year Silver Mining Suppression — 'Silver Cross' Signal Points To Next Leg Of Precious Metals Cycle
snippet: Weiss Ratings analyst Sean Brodrick argues that the 'Silver Cross' signal — when silver begins outpacing gold on a relative basis — has now been triggered, ending a nine-year suppression of silver mining equities. The firm carries a $6,900 gold price outlook and positions mining stocks as the primary leveraged vehicle for the cycle's next leg.

headline: GDX vs. SLVP: Silver Miners WIN The 1-Year Race But Gold Miners Hold The Steadier Long Game — April 2026 ETF Breakdown
snippet: With GDX up 108% YoY and SLVP outperforming on a 1-year basis, Motley Fool breaks down the trade-offs: SLVP (top holdings: Hecla, Fresnillo, Penoles) offers higher yield and industrial leverage, while GDX (Agnico Eagle, Newmont, Barrick) provides lower beta and five-year outperformance.
